response.justification: Bogdan is a technically versatile senior/staff individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and some relevant developer-tooling work (CI/CD setup, test frameworks, MCP generators), but he shows no evidence of people management, team leadership, performance reviews, or organizational change — all of which are core requirements for this mid-to-senior management role. The position demands 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of technical leadership over others; his resume describes no direct reports, no team ownership, and no roadmap or org-level strategy work.
strengths: Broad technical depth across multiple stacks and paradigms, including developer tooling (CI/CD, test runners, MCP frameworks) that aligns with the team's focus area
Demonstrated engineering craftsmanship culture (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, workshops) consistent with a productivity-focused team's values
Long career with cross-domain exposure (mobile, embedded, web, data) providing useful context for supporting a diverse engineering org
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance reviews, or 1:1s
No evidence of setting team priorities, owning a technical roadmap, or driving organizational change at any scope
Developer tooling contributions are incidental side-of-desk work (CI server setup, open-source libraries) rather than a sustained, primary focus of 3+ years
